guardrail_evaluations.explanation: The effective discount of 33.3% is well above the 20% soft ceiling for mid-market deals. This is a yellow flag that the AE should be prepared to justify in any internal review.
At 33.3% discount, this deal exceeds the 30% mid-market hard threshold and cannot proceed without explicit Finance sign-off. The AE must escalate before re-quoting or accepting terms.

The ramp schedule covers 12 months (months 1–12), far exceeding the 3-month mid-market ceiling. This structure obscures effective discount and creates ASC 606 complexity — Finance approval is required before this structure can be accepted.
Estimated gross margin on this deal is approximately 10%, well below the 40% warn threshold. The combination of discount, ramp, and waived services is severely compressing margin.
At an estimated 10% gross margin, this deal violates the absolute 25% floor and must be restructured before any quote is issued. The deal cannot close in its current form.

alternative_structures.label: 36-Month Commit with Reduced Cash Discount
alternative_structures.description: Extend term to 36 months, reduce list discount from 33.3% to 20%, eliminate the 6-month free period and replace it with a 3-month ramp (months 1–3 at 50% of ARR). Retain waived implementation as the goodwill concession. Remove the 90-day out clause — a 36-month commit is incompatible with anytime termination.
alternative_structures.rationale: The customer gets consolidation savings and vendor lock-in protection (expansion pricing lock); Clay gets a longer committed term that de-risks churn and restores margin to the 40% floor. The trade-off is removing the out clause, which requires CRO buy-in from Tessera — but the new CRO's consolidation mandate makes a multi-year bet on one platform strategically sensible.

alternative_structures.label: Year-1 Ramp + Year-2 Escalator (No Free Period)
alternative_structures.description: Remove the 6-month free period entirely. Replace with a genuine ramp: Y1 ACV at $252K (30% discount), Y2 ACV at $306K (15% discount), paid annually upfront. Total TCV ~$558K over 24 months vs. $480K today. Cap ramp at 3 months (months 1–3 at 70% of Y1 rate) to satisfy the mid-market ramp guardrail. Retain waived implementation. Remove expansion pricing lock — the escalator already provides year-over-year predictability.
alternative_structures.rationale: This structure replaces disguised discounting (free months) with transparent, Finance-compliant ramp economics. Y1 discount is at the Finance sign-off boundary; Y2 steps up automatically, improving blended TCV and signaling long-term partnership. The escalator gives Tessera's new CRO budget predictability without a pricing freeze that disadvantages Clay.

alternative_structures.label: Implementation Unbundled + Smaller Cash Discount + Co-Marketing Credit
alternative_structures.description: Re-bundle the $40K implementation services as a billable line item at full margin. Reduce cash discount to 25% ($270K ACV). Offer a $20K co-marketing credit (case study, joint webinar, reference calls) in lieu of any further discount. Maintain 24-month term. Shorten ramp to 3 months at 50% ramp rate (months 1–3) to satisfy guardrail. Remove 90-day out clause; offer a 12-month out after month 18 as a compromise.
alternative_structures.rationale: Unbundling professional services recovers a full-margin $40K revenue line that improves blended economics. The co-marketing credit costs Clay near zero but has high perceived value to a Series C company trying to build enterprise credibility. The 12-month out after month 18 gives Tessera's CRO a safety valve without the open-ended 90-day clause that creates churn risk from day one.

pricing.reasoning_summary: This deal is deeply distressed across every margin and discount metric. The effective discount is 33.3% — above the mid-market block_without_approval threshold of 30% — and the 12-month ramp structure (6 months at $0, then 6 months ramping) violates the 3-month mid-market ramp guardrail, also at block_without_approval severity. Most critically, the estimated gross margin is approximately 10%, which breaches the absolute 25% floor (block_absolute) and cannot proceed in its current form. Margin is calculated assuming list price carries a 40% gross margin (i.e., COGS = 60% × $360K = $216K), which the CFO should validate — actual COGS may differ. The non-standard clauses (90-day out, 2026 expansion pricing lock through 2027, $40K waived implementation) compound the economics further and are not captured in the margin estimate. Precedent deals provided were enterprise-tier at 11–16% discounts and are not structurally comparable; they were not used in this analysis.

performance_obligations.name: Platform subscription (paid term)
performance_obligations.description: 24-month subscription access to the platform, with 6 months free followed by 18 months paid at $20K/month.
performance_obligations.rationale: Subscription SaaS access is a series of distinct services satisfied over time; customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefit (ASC 606-10-25-27).

performance_obligations.name: Free-period platform access (Months 1-6)
performance_obligations.description: Six months of free platform access prior to the paid term commencing.
performance_obligations.rationale: The 6 free months are not a separate PO but a material right/discount embedded in the same subscription service; total contract consideration ($480K TCV / $240K ACV interpretation) should be allocated ratably across the full 24-month service period.

performance_obligations.name: Implementation services
performance_obligations.description: Implementation services normally priced at $40K, waived in this deal.
performance_obligations.rationale: Although implementation could be capable of being distinct, when bundled and waived it is not separately identifiable from the subscription; treat as part of the combined performance obligation and recognize ratably.

variable_consideration_flags.explanation: The 6-month free period followed by paid months creates a ramp. Total fixed consideration is known ($480K TCV across 24 months) and should be recognized straight-line over the full service period, not on the billing cadence.

A 90-day anytime out clause may shorten the enforceable contract term under ASC 606-10-25-3 to effectively a rolling 90-day contract, which would compress revenue recognition and re-characterize TCV. Requires legal/accounting determination of whether substantive termination penalties exist.

The 2026-priced seat expansion lock through 2027 may constitute a material right under ASC 606-10-55-42 if priced below standalone selling price, requiring allocation of transaction price to the option.

contract_modification_risk.explanation: Both the 90-day TFC and the expansion pricing lock create modification risk. Exercise of TFC would trigger a contract modification/termination accounting event. Seat expansions at locked 2026 pricing in 2027 could trigger modification accounting under ASC 606-10-25-10 if not treated as a separate contract.

recognized_revenue_schedule.notes: Straight-line allocation of $480K TCV over 24 months = $20K/month recognized despite zero billing.
$20K/month recognized; billed $120K annual upfront covers this period.
$20K/month ratable; billed $120K (months 13-24) — assumes full 24-month enforceable term.
Remaining ratable allocation if TCV is $480K across 24 months. If enforceable term is shorter due to TFC, schedule must be revised.

red_flags.label: 90-day termination for convenience may shorten enforceable contract term
red_flags.explanation: Without substantive termination penalties, ASC 606 may require treating this as a rolling 90-day contract, materially changing revenue recognition and disqualifying multi-year TCV recognition. CFO/auditor pre-clearance required.

red_flags.label: Expansion pricing lock through 2027 — material right
red_flags.explanation: The locked 2026 pricing for 2027 expansions likely represents a material right requiring transaction price allocation to the option, deferring a portion of revenue.

red_flags.label: 6 months free + waived $40K implementation
red_flags.explanation: Significant non-cash consideration must be allocated ratably across the service period; cannot be recognized on a billing-cadence basis. Effective discount stack pushes ACV well below list.

red_flags.label: TCV/ACV reconciliation
red_flags.explanation: Stated ACV $240K and TCV $480K imply 24 months of $20K/month paid, but only 18 months are billed. Confirm total contractual consideration to ensure schedule sums correctly.

asc606.reasoning_summary: This deal stacks multiple variable-consideration and modification-risk features: a 6-month free ramp, waived implementation, a 90-day TFC, and a multi-year expansion pricing lock. The TFC in particular may compress the enforceable contract term and undermine ratable recognition of the full TCV, while the pricing lock likely constitutes a material right requiring transaction-price allocation. This is an automated estimate; final determination requires CFO/auditor review.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests the right to terminate the agreement for any reason upon 90 days' written notice, exercisable at any time during the 24-month term.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A 90-day anytime TFC on a 24-month deal means Tessera can exit after month 9 (6 free + 90-day notice) having paid zero dollars — Clay absorbs the full 6-month free ramp, $40K waived implementation, and 35% discount concession with no revenue recovery. The mechanism: notice served on day 1 of month 7 (first paid month) results in a final paid month at month 9, yielding only $60K collected against $480K TCV.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Section [X] — Termination for Convenience: Customer may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' prior written notice, provided that (a) such termination right shall not be exercisable prior to the end of month eighteen (18) of the Subscription Term ('TFC Eligibility Date'), and (b) in the event Customer exercises this right, Customer shall remain liable for all fees accrued through the effective termination date plus a termination fee equal to three (3) months of the then-current monthly subscription fee. For clarity, no termination-for-convenience right exists prior to the TFC Eligibility Date; termination before month 18 shall be governed solely by the Termination for Cause provisions herein.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: TFC permitted no earlier than month 15, with a 60-day notice period and a termination fee equal to two (2) months of the then-current monthly subscription fee. No TFC before month 12 under any circumstance given the 6-month free ramp already granted.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has previously accepted TFC with a 12-month lock-in floor on deals with comparable ramp concessions; an anytime TFC is not known to have been approved.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests that any seat expansions executed through end of calendar year 2027 be priced at 2026 contracted rates, regardless of then-current list or negotiated pricing.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A hard pricing lock through December 2027 on an uncapped seat count means Tessera can expand aggressively — especially during their headcount growth phase targeting enterprise — at a rate already discounted 35% from list. If Clay raises prices in 2027 (likely given product expansion), the expansion lock caps revenue upside precisely when Tessera's growth compounds the volume. The deal's health score of 58 further signals possible churn pressure, making a locked low-price expansion right asymmetrically risky: Tessera expands when convenient, churns when not.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Section [X] — Expansion Pricing: Expansion seats purchased during the initial Subscription Term shall be priced at the then-current applicable rate for Customer's tier at time of order, subject to a maximum annual price increase of five percent (5%) over the 2026 contracted per-seat rate. This pricing assurance (a) applies solely to seat-count expansions of up to fifty percent (50%) above the contracted seat count at signing ('Expansion Cap'), (b) expires upon the end of the initial Subscription Term or December 31, 2027, whichever is earlier, and (c) requires that Customer not be in breach of any payment obligations at time of expansion order.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept a 2026 rate lock capped at 25% seat expansion above baseline, expiring at the earlier of term end or June 30, 2027, with a 3% annual escalator applied to the locked rate from January 1, 2027 onward.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has offered expansion rate holds in competitive displacements but typically scoped to 12 months and 20–30% volume ceiling; a 24-month uncapped lock has not been approved.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: The first six (6) months of the 24-month subscription term are free of charge, with the paid term commencing at month 7, resulting in Clay collecting fees for only 18 of 24 months.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Combined with the 35% discount already applied to list ($360K → $240K ACV), a 6-month free period reduces effective collected revenue to $360K over 24 months (18 paid months × $20K/month), against a list value of $720K TCV — an effective 50% haircut. Stacked with the waived $40K implementation fee and a 90-day anytime TFC, Clay's worst-case collected revenue drops to $60K (3 paid months before a month-7 TFC notice resolves). The free period alone is not unusual for displacements but must not coexist with an anytime TFC.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Section [X] — Ramp Credit Period: Clay shall provide Customer with a six (6) month onboarding credit period ('Credit Period') commencing on the Effective Date, during which no subscription fees are due. The paid Subscription Term of twenty-four (24) months shall commence on the first day of month seven (7) ('Paid Term Commencement Date'). In consideration of the Credit Period, Customer agrees that (a) the Agreement may not be terminated for convenience prior to month eighteen (18) of the total term (inclusive of the Credit Period), and (b) no refunds or credits shall be issued for the Credit Period under any termination scenario.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Retain the 6-month free period as proposed, conditional on removal of any TFC right before month 15 of the total term and explicit acknowledgment that the Credit Period months do not count toward any TFC notice calculation.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: 6-month free ramps have been approved on competitive displacement deals at similar ACV levels; the issue is the concurrent TFC, not the ramp itself.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests that Clay waive the standard $40,000 implementation services fee in full as a condition of closing.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Waiving $40K in implementation services on a $240K ACV deal represents a 16.7% additional concession on top of the 35% discount, pushing total economic concession to roughly 48% of list value. More operationally, unpaid implementations reduce the services team's incentive to prioritize Tessera's onboarding and leave Clay with no contractual hook to enforce scope — any scope creep becomes unfunded cost. With a health score of 58 and a possible Series D fundraise looming, Tessera faces capital efficiency pressure that could make them demanding customers without a services contract anchoring expectations.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Section [X] — Implementation Services: Clay shall provide implementation services valued at $40,000 ('Implementation Services') to Customer at no additional charge, subject to the following conditions: (a) Implementation Services shall be scoped per Clay's standard Statement of Work attached hereto as Exhibit A; (b) any Customer-requested work outside the defined scope shall be quoted and billed at Clay's then-current professional services rate; (c) Implementation Services must be scheduled and commenced within ninety (90) days of the Effective Date, after which any unused implementation credit shall expire with no cash or credit value.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept the full waiver but attach a one-page Scope of Work limiting implementation to a defined deliverable list (e.g., CRM integration, user provisioning, 2 training sessions) with a hard expiry date, providing scope control even without revenue.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Implementation fee waivers have been approved on competitive displacements; scoping the SOW is standard practice when waiving fees.

standard_clauses_affirmed: Data residency: not flagged, assumed standard US processing
Indemnification: not flagged, assumed mutual standard
Warranty: not flagged, assumed standard 90-day SLA
redline.one_line_summary: We cannot ship this deal as-is — the 90-day anytime TFC stacked on a 6-month free ramp creates a scenario where Tessera exits after paying $60K on a $480K TCV; we need TFC moved to month 18, the expansion lock capped at 50% seats with a 5% escalator, and the implementation waiver scoped to a defined SOW.
redline.reasoning_summary: The headline risk is the combination of a 6-month free period and an anytime 90-day TFC, which together allow Tessera to exit the deal after paying as little as three months of fees — approximately $60K against $480K TCV and $40K in waived services. The expansion pricing lock compounds the risk by capping Clay's upside on a customer signaling strong headcount growth and consolidation intent. While Tessera's consolidation motivation is genuine (new CRO, VP RevOps RFP, profitable quarters), their health score of 58 and Series D capital pressure mean churn risk is non-trivial, making an uncapped, unlocked TFC a dealbreaker. All four non-standard clauses require modification before this deal can be approved.

required_approvers.rationale: TCV of $480K is below $1M threshold, but discount of 33.3% exceeds the 25% rule and margin estimate of 10% is critically low.
Effective discount of 33.3% (per Pricing Agent) exceeds the 25% threshold and triggers CFO review for pricing exception.
ACV of $240K falls within the $100K–$500K range requiring RevOps structural review.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Redline priority block — multiple non-standard clauses
required_approvers.rationale: Redline Agent flagged overall_redline_priority as 'block' due to 90-day TFC stacked on 6-month free period, expansion pricing lock, waived implementation, and termination-for-convenience risk creating unacceptable churn/revenue collapse scenario.

blockers_to_address_first: Pricing Agent flagged 'block_absolute' on gross margin floor — deal violates 25% absolute margin floor at estimated 10% margin and cannot proceed in current form without restructure per Pricing Agent.
Pricing Agent flagged 'block_without_approval' on mid-market ramp length — 12-month ramp (months 1–12) exceeds 3-month mid-market ceiling and requires Finance sign-off before re-quoting.

Redline Agent flagged 'block' overall priority — 90-day anytime TFC combined with 6-month free period allows Tessera to exit after paying ~$60K on $480K TCV. TFC must be moved to month 18 minimum with termination fee or deal cannot close.

ASC 606 Agent flagged 'block_without_approval' on 90-day TFC — termination right may shorten enforceable contract term, collapsing revenue recognition schedule and disqualifying multi-year TCV recognition without CFO/auditor pre-clearance.
Expansion pricing lock (2026 rates through 2027) requires legal/accounting treatment as material right under ASC 606 and must be capped by volume/time per Redline counter-proposal.

AE must obtain Pricing Agent's sign-off on one of three alternative structures (36-month with 20% discount; Y1-Y2 ramp with escalator; unbundled implementation + 25% discount) before resubmitting — current deal structure is non-viable.
approval.one_line_summary: This deal is blocked on three fronts — Pricing Agent rejects it at 10% margin (absolute floor violation), Redline flags the 90-day TFC + 6-month free stack as unacceptable churn risk, and ASC 606 requires CFO clearance on the TFC's impact on revenue recognition — AE must restructure (per Pricing alternatives), move TFC to month 18+, and cap the expansion lock before it routes through AE Manager → CFO + RevOps + Legal (parallel); expect 7 days minimum if AE resolves blockers.
approval.reasoning_summary: The deal fires four approval rules: CFO (both discount >25% and implicit margin concern), RevOps (ACV $100K–$500K), and Legal (redline block priority). The Pricing Agent's block_absolute margin violation and block_without_approval ramp-length/discount thresholds, combined with Redline's block overall priority and ASC 606's block_without_approval on the 90-day TFC, make this unapprovalable in its current form. The approval chain is CFO + RevOps + Legal in parallel (step 2) after AE Manager (step 1), but none can approve until the AE restructures the deal per Pricing guidance, removes or materially constrains the TFC per Redline counters, and obtains CFO/auditor confirmation of revenue recognition treatment per ASC 606.

text.text: Deal Review: Apollo/Outreach/ZoomInfo Displacement — Tessera Health

fields.text:
*Approval Chain*
AE Mgr → CFO + RevOps + Legal (parallel) | ~7 days

text.text:
*What the deal team needs to know:*

• *Deal is blocked in current form — three hard stops.* Gross margin is ~10% against an absolute 25% floor (block_absolute), the 12-month ramp violates the 3-month mid-market ceiling (block_without_approval), and the redline agent flagged overall priority as block. AE must restructure before resubmission — Pricing Agent has provided three alternative structures (36-month at 20% discount; Y1/Y2 ramp with escalator; unbundled implementation at 25% discount).

• *The 90-day anytime TFC is the single most dangerous clause.* Stacked on a 6-month free period, it allows Tessera to exit after paying as little as $60K on $480K TCV. Redline requires TFC moved to no earlier than month 18, with a termination fee. ASC 606 flags the same clause as a potential contract-term compressor requiring CFO/auditor pre-clearance before any revenue recognition schedule can be relied on.

• *Strategic fit is real but the concession stack is unsustainable.* New CRO hired for consolidation, VP RevOps RFP underway, two profitable quarters — the buying intent is genuine. However, 35% cash discount + 6 months free + $40K waived implementation + uncapped 2027 pricing lock amounts to ~50% of list value given away. Health score of 58 and Series D capital pressure add churn risk. Tessera is worth winning; this structure is not the way.

elements.text: AE: Priya Shankar | AE Manager: Marcus Reilly | Close target: 2026-05-14 | review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

slack_post.plaintext_fallback:
Deal Review: Apollo/Outreach/ZoomInfo Displacement — Tessera Health (Mid-Market)
ACV $240K | TCV $480K | 33.3% effective discount | ~10% gross margin (absolute floor: 25%) | Approval: AE Mgr → CFO + RevOps + Legal (~7 days)
DEAL IS BLOCKED: Margin violates absolute floor, 12-month ramp exceeds guardrail, and 90-day anytime TFC stacked on 6-month free period creates a scenario where Tessera exits after paying ~$60K on $480K TCV.
AE must restructure per Pricing Agent alternatives, move TFC to month 18+, and obtain CFO/auditor clearance on ASC 606 revenue recognition before resubmission.
AE: Priya Shankar | Manager: Marcus Reilly | review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

ae_email_draft.subject: [Tessera Health] Deal blocked — 3 structural fixes required before approval can route
ae_email_draft.body_markdown:
Priya — pricing review is complete and this deal is **blocked in its current form** across three independent guardrails: 10% gross margin violates the 25% absolute floor, the 90-day anytime TFC stacked on the 6-month free ramp creates a scenario where Tessera exits having paid ~$60K on a $480K TCV, and the 12-month ramp structure exceeds the 3-month mid-market ceiling requiring Finance pre-clearance. You cannot re-quote or route for approval until these are resolved.

**Before resubmitting, you must do all three of the following:**

1. **Restructure pricing to clear the margin floor.** Choose one of the three Pricing-approved alternatives — the 36-month/20% discount structure restores margin to 40% and is the cleanest path; the Y1/Y2 ramp escalator is the best option if Tessera won't budge on term. Current structure (35% discount + 6 months free + waived implementation) is non-viable at any approval level.
2. **Move the TFC to month 18 minimum with a termination fee.** An anytime 90-day out is a dealbreaker — Legal has provided exact counter-language (TFC eligible no earlier than month 18, 3-month fee on exit). If Tessera pushes back, the fallback floor is month 15 with a 2-month fee. No TFC before month 12 under any scenario given the free ramp already granted.
3. **Cap the expansion pricing lock.** Redline's counter limits it to 50% seat expansion above baseline with a 5% annual escalator and expiry at the earlier of term end or December 31, 2027. This also resolves the ASC 606 material right flag — get Legal to confirm treatment once scoped.

Once restructured, the approval chain is: **(1) Marcus Reilly** → **(2) CFO + RevOps + Legal in parallel** — budget a minimum of 7 business days from clean resubmission, which puts you at the edge of your May 14 close date. Escalate to Marcus today so he can pre-brief the CFO and Legal ahead of your resubmit.

customer_email_draft.subject: Tessera / Clay — revised terms framework ahead of May 14 decision
customer_email_draft.body_markdown:
Thanks for the time this week — we understand Tessera is under real pressure to close a vendor decision before mid-May, and we want to make that timeline work. That said, I need to be straight with you on where we can and can't move before we get internal sign-off on a revised order form.

On the six-month credit period: we can keep it. The onboarding ramp is a genuine concession we're prepared to stand behind for a competitive displacement of this size. What we cannot accept is a 90-day termination-for-convenience right running concurrently — that combination means Clay absorbs the full ramp cost with no committed revenue as a floor. Our counter is a TFC right that becomes exercisable at month 18 of the total term, with 90 days' notice and a modest wind-down fee; that gives your CRO a real exit valve while giving us a protected base period that reflects the concession we're already making.

On expansion pricing: we're open to a rate hold for seat expansions, but we need two guardrails — a volume cap of 50% above your contracted seat count at signing, and a 5% annual escalator applied from January 1, 2027 onward on the locked rate. An uncapped, unescalated freeze through December 2027 isn't something we can approve given where our product roadmap is heading.

On implementation services: the $40K waiver stays, but we'll need a defined Scope of Work attached to the order form — standard CRM integration, user provisioning, and two training sessions — with a 90-day commencement window. This protects both sides on scope and keeps our services team accountable to your timeline.

On pricing: the current structure requires Finance and Legal sign-off before we can issue a revised quote; I'm escalating today and will have a redlined order form back to you by end of week. The fastest path to your May 14 deadline is alignment on these four points in the next 48 hours so our internal review runs in parallel with your evaluation. Can we get 30 minutes Thursday to confirm?

approval_review_one_pager.title: Apollo/Outreach/ZoomInfo Displacement — Tessera Health Deal Review

sections.heading: Headline
sections.content_markdown: Tessera Health (Series C, 1,200 employees, Healthtech) seeks to consolidate a $320K/yr Apollo + Outreach + ZoomInfo stack onto Clay at $240K ACV / $480K TCV over 24 months. The proposed structure — 35% discount, 6 months free, $40K waived implementation, 90-day anytime termination for convenience, and a 2026 expansion pricing lock through 2027 — violates three hard pricing guardrails and is blocked in current form.

sections.heading: Pricing Summary
sections.content_markdown:
**List price:** $360,000 ACV | **Proposed price:** $240,000 ACV | **Effective discount:** 33.3% (above 30% hard block threshold)

**Estimated gross margin:** ~10% — violates the **25% absolute floor** (block_absolute). At 40% list-price margin, COGS ~$216K against $240K ACV leaves virtually no room.

**Concession stack (full economic hit):**
- 33.3% cash discount: -$120K vs. list
- 6-month free ramp: -$120K collected (18 of 24 months billed)
- Waived implementation: -$40K
- **Worst-case collected revenue (90-day TFC exercised at month 7):** ~$60K on $480K TCV

**Alternative structures proposed:**
1. **36-month commit, 20% discount** — $288K ACV, ~40% margin; remove TFC
2. **Y1/Y2 ramp + escalator** — $252K Y1 / $306K Y2, ~30% blended margin; no free period
3. **Unbundle implementation + 25% discount + $20K co-marketing credit** — $270K ACV, ~33% margin; TFC after month 18 only

sections.heading: Risk Findings
sections.content_markdown:
**BLOCK — ABSOLUTE (cannot close as-is):**
- Gross margin ~10% breaches the 25% absolute floor. Deal must be restructured before any quote is issued.

**BLOCK WITHOUT APPROVAL:**
- Discount of 33.3% exceeds the 30% mid-market Finance sign-off threshold.
- 12-month ramp structure (months 1-6 at $0, months 7-12 partial) far exceeds the 3-month mid-market ramp ceiling; obscures effective discount and triggers ASC 606 complexity.
- 90-day anytime TFC may shorten the enforceable contract term to a rolling 90-day agreement under ASC 606-10-25-3, collapsing multi-year TCV recognition — CFO/auditor pre-clearance required.

**REDLINE BLOCK (overall priority: BLOCK):**
- TFC + free ramp stack: Tessera can serve notice on day 1 of month 7 and exit after paying only ~$60K. Counter: TFC not exercisable before month 18, plus 3-month termination fee.
- Expansion pricing lock (2026 rates through 2027, uncapped seats): caps Clay's upside during Tessera's headcount growth phase. Counter: 50% seat expansion cap, 5% annual escalator, expiry at term end or Dec 31, 2027.
- $40K implementation waiver: 16.7% additional effective concession; attach SOW with scope cap and 90-day commencement deadline.

**ASC 606 WARNINGS:**
- 2026 expansion pricing lock likely constitutes a material right under ASC 606-10-55-42, requiring transaction price allocation and partial revenue deferral.
- 6-month free period + waived implementation must be recognized ratably over the full 24-month period, not on billing cadence.
- If TFC is retained, revenue recognition schedule must be rebuilt on a rolling 90-day basis — materially changes reported TCV.

**CUSTOMER RISK FLAGS:**
- Health score: 58 (below average). Series D capital efficiency pressure may make Tessera a demanding, cost-sensitive customer.
- Uncapped anytime TFC on a discounted deal with a distressed health score = asymmetric churn risk.

sections.heading: Approval Routing
sections.content_markdown:
**AE must resolve all blockers before routing. Current deal is non-approvable.**

| Step | Approver | Role | Veto? |
|------|----------|------|---------|
| 1 | Marcus Reilly | AE Manager | No |
| 2 (parallel) | CFO | Discount >25%, margin violation | **Yes** |
| 2 (parallel) | RevOps | ACV $100K-$500K structural review | No |
| 2 (parallel) | Legal | Redline block — multiple non-standard clauses | **Yes** |

**Estimated cycle time:** 7 business days after AE resolves blockers.

**Pre-routing checklist for AE (Priya Shankar):**
1. Restructure deal per one of the three Pricing Agent alternatives.
2. Move TFC to month 18 minimum + attach termination fee.
3. Cap expansion pricing lock (volume + 5% escalator).
4. Attach SOW to implementation waiver.
5. Obtain CFO/auditor pre-clearance on ASC 606 treatment of TFC.

sections.heading: Recommendation
sections.content_markdown:
**HOLD — restructure required before routing.**

This deal cannot be approved in its current form: it violates the absolute gross margin floor (10% vs. 25% minimum), fires two block-without-approval pricing guardrails, carries a redline block due to the TFC + free ramp stack, and requires CFO/auditor pre-clearance on ASC 606 revenue recognition. The strategic fit is real — Tessera's consolidation intent is credible and well-signaled — but the AE must return with a restructured proposal (recommend the Y1/Y2 ramp + escalator or the 36-month commit) with TFC moved to month 18+ before any approval chain can run.

comms.reasoning_summary: Calibrated customer email to firm tone given the deal context. AE email leads with verdict (ship-with-conditions (blockers must clear first)) and a numbered next-step list. One-pager and Slack post both headline the recommendation and surface 4 flagged redlines (4 countered in customer reply). Approval routing summary: ~7 business days through 4-step chain.

synthesis: Pricing is a hard stop: the deal carries a 33.3% effective discount and an estimated 10% gross margin, breaching the absolute 25% margin floor on top of a 12-month ramp that blows the 3-month mid-market guardrail. ASC 606 review flags four variable-consideration issues, with the 90-day TFC likely compressing the enforceable term and the multi-year pricing lock probably constituting a material right requiring transaction-price reallocation. Redline rates this an overall block — the 6-month free period plus an anytime 90-day TFC let Tessera walk after roughly $60K paid against $480K TCV and $40K of waived services, so the TFC must be capped or removed. Approval fires CFO, RevOps, and Legal in parallel behind AE Manager (~7 business days), and none can sign until the AE restructures pricing, constrains the TFC, and secures CFO/auditor sign-off on rev rec. Recommendation: hold and re-scope before any further customer commitment, communicating in a firm tone with the four redline counters on the table.

signals.headline: Tessera Health names new Chief Revenue Officer to lead consolidation push
signals.summary: Tessera Health announced the appointment of a new CRO (formerly VP Sales at a rival healthtech provider), tasked with consolidating sales tooling and accelerating mid-market enterprise expansion through 2026.

signals.headline: Tessera Health expands HIPAA-attested integration suite for hospital networks
signals.summary: Tessera released expanded HIPAA-attested integrations targeting Epic, Cerner, and Meditech-shaped buyers. The release is positioned as evidence of compliance maturity ahead of Tessera's enterprise upmarket push.

signals.headline: Tessera Health crosses 1,200 employees, posts second consecutive profitable quarter
signals.summary: Q4 2025 earnings update notes a 1,200-person headcount and a second straight profitable quarter, alongside a stated 2026 priority of vendor consolidation across go-to-market infrastructure.

signals.headline: Tessera Health adds VP RevOps from data-platform vendor
signals.summary: Tessera hired a VP RevOps from a data-platform vendor; her stated 90-day plan reportedly includes a sales-tooling RFP focused on prospecting, enrichment, and outbound orchestration.

signals.headline: Healthtech RevOps leaders cite stack consolidation as #1 2026 priority — survey
signals.summary: Industry survey finds 64% of mid-market healthtech RevOps leaders plan to consolidate three or more sales-engagement tools in 2026, citing renewal pressure and security-review fatigue.
